Container.Name: llama-swap
Container.Overview: Tiny reverse proxy that hot-swaps llama.cpp models on demand. Define many GGUFs in one config.yaml; llama-swap spawns the right llama-server process when a request names a model, keeps it warm, and unloads idle ones on a TTL. Supersedes the llama-server template for any setup with more than one model. [br]Official image bundles llama-swap + llama.cpp:server-vulkan in one container, so Vulkan GPU acceleration works out of the box on AMD (no ROCm), Intel Arc, and NVIDIA. [br]Drop your GGUFs into the Models dir, edit /config/config.yaml with one entry per model, and point any OpenAI-compatible client at http://UNRAID-IP:8080.
Container.PostArgs: -c "f=/config/config.yaml; mkdir -p /config; if [ ! -f $f ]; then wget -qO $f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iknox/unraid-templates/main/config-examples/llama-swap.config.yaml; echo '[init] seeded config.yaml'; fi; exec /app/llama-swap -config /config/config.yaml -watch-config"
Container.Description: llama-swap is a lightweight YAML-driven proxy that forks llama-server processes on demand. One HTTP listener, many models, automatic unload on idle. The vulkan tag bundles ggml-org/llama.cpp:server-vulkan underneath so no extra inference container is needed. [br]With -watch-config, edits to config.yaml reload live — no container restart to add or tune models.

Config.Name: HTTP / API
Config.Description: OpenAI-compatible API plus llama-swap's own /logs, /running, /unload endpoints. llama-server children bind to internal ports starting at 5800.

Config.Name: Models
Config.Description: GGUF files referenced by config.yaml. Shared with the llama-server template by default so the same models work in both.

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: Holds config.yaml. See the repo's config.example.yaml for the full schema. With -watch-config, edits hot-reload the model list.
